verses.text: Hear what the LORD says; He has 'a controversy with his people,' pleading before the mountains. A divine lawsuit. God reasons with His people over their ingratitude.
Hear what the LORD says; He has 'a controversy with his people,' pleading before the mountains. A divine lawsuit. God reasons with His people over their ingratitude.
'O my people, what have I done unto thee?' He recalls the exodus, redemption, and Balaam's frustrated curse. Mercies rehearsed. God's kindness, not His harshness, is set before them.
'O my people, what have I done unto thee?' He recalls the exodus, redemption, and Balaam's frustrated curse. Mercies rehearsed. God's kindness, not His harshness, is set before them.
'O my people, what have I done unto thee?' He recalls the exodus, redemption, and Balaam's frustrated curse. Mercies rehearsed. God's kindness, not His harshness, is set before them.
Wherewith shall I come before the LORD — with burnt offerings, thousands of rams, or my firstborn? Misguided zeal. Men imagine costly offerings can buy what God truly wants.
Wherewith shall I come before the LORD — with burnt offerings, thousands of rams, or my firstborn? Misguided zeal. Men imagine costly offerings can buy what God truly wants.
'He hath shewed thee, O man, what is good... to do justly, and to love mercy, and to walk humbly with thy God.' The heart of religion. God seeks justice, mercy, and humility, not mere ceremony.
The LORD cries to the city: are there still 'the treasures of wickedness,' the scant measure, and false weights? Dishonest gain. God hates the cheating that wrongs the poor.
The LORD cries to the city: are there still 'the treasures of wickedness,' the scant measure, and false weights? Dishonest gain. God hates the cheating that wrongs the poor.

The LORD cries to the city: are there still 'the treasures of wickedness,' the scant measure, and false weights? Dishonest gain. God hates the cheating that wrongs the poor.
Therefore He will smite them; they shall sow but not reap, for they kept 'the statutes of Omri.' Fruitless toil. Following wicked example brings emptiness and reproach.
